explanation: Story IB2-116 states that users should be able to resize the Image Widget using a 'Width slider from Nav Panel'. In direct contradiction, Story IB2-308 explicitly states that 'The Image Widget's width must not be changeable from the Nav Panel Width slider' and 'Users must resize the Image Widget only by dragging the dotted outline directly on the page'. These two stories propose mutually exclusive methods for resizing the same UI component.
suggested_fix: Product Management must decide on a single, consistent method for resizing the Image Widget. Either enable the Nav Panel Width slider (align with IB2-116) or disable it and enforce direct dragging (align with IB2-308). One of these stories should be modified or removed to resolve the conflict.

explanation: Story IB2-117 states that the image should 're-position and see the Image in the same percentage of ‘Block’ after resizing', implying proportional positioning. Story IB2-308 directly contradicts this by stating 'When the Image Widget is resized, the image should reset to the center of its Block instead of preserving the same horizontal percentage position'. These are mutually exclusive behaviors for image positioning after resizing.
suggested_fix: Clarify the desired behavior for image positioning within its block after resizing. Decide whether the image should maintain its proportional position or reset to the center, and update both stories to reflect the agreed-upon rule.

explanation: Story IB2-15 states that when 'Allow User updates to Titles upon creation' is disabled, child records are created and an info snackbar appears. Story IB2-20, under the same condition ('no end user customisation is enabled'), states that if the child database's title field is invalid, no child records are created and no snackbar error messaging is needed. These are contradictory outcomes (child records created vs. not created, snackbar appears vs. no snackbar) for the same initial configuration state.
suggested_fix: Clarify the exact conditions under which child records are created versus not created when 'Allow User updates to Titles upon creation' is disabled. Specifically, define how an invalid child database title field (as per IB2-20) interacts with the success path described in IB2-15. The stories must provide a consistent outcome for this shared scenario.

explanation: Story IB2-164 states the 'Drag & Drop Page limit' should be set 'via Custom Page limit in MCP' and 'shown somewhere on Custom Page' or in the 'Restrictions tab in MCP or Custom Page'. In direct contradiction, Story IB2-389 explicitly states 'I want to set Page limits via MCP settings, not in Custom Page'. This creates a conflict regarding the intended location for configuring page limits.
suggested_fix: Clarify the definitive location for setting 'Page limits' and 'Drag & Drop Page limits'. Determine if these settings should be exclusively within MCP settings or if they can be configured/displayed on the Custom Page itself. Align both stories to a single, consistent approach.

explanation: Story IB2-78 states that upon a failed server deletion, a snackbar error message should appear and the dialog should remain open. Story IB2-172 directly contradicts this by stating that no snackbar error message should be displayed and the confirmation dialog must close automatically on a failed server deletion. These are mutually exclusive requirements for the exact same scenario.
suggested_fix: Product Management needs to clarify the desired behavior for failed server deletions: whether a snackbar should appear and if the dialog should remain open or close. Update one of the stories to align with the chosen behavior.

explanation: Story IB2-189 requires the image position within its Block area to remain proportional after resizing. In direct contradiction, Story IB2-308 states that when the Image Widget is resized, the image should reset to the center of its Block instead of preserving the same horizontal percentage position. These are mutually exclusive behaviors for image positioning after a resize operation.
suggested_fix: Clarify the desired behavior for image positioning after resizing. Decide whether the image should maintain proportional placement (IB2-189) or reset to the center (IB2-308), and update one of the stories accordingly.

explanation: IB2-190 states that the image should maintain the 'same percentage horizontally' with its block across different responsive modes after resizing/repositioning. In direct contradiction, IB2-308 specifies that 'the image should reset to the center of its Block instead of preserving the same horizontal percentage position' when resized. These two requirements for image positioning behavior are mutually exclusive.
suggested_fix: Clarify the desired behavior for image positioning after resizing. Determine whether the image should maintain its horizontal percentage position or reset to the center of its block, and update one or both stories to reflect a consistent rule.

explanation: IB2-215 explicitly states 'This ticket does NOT include drag-and-drop across Parent', meaning pages cannot be moved to a different parent or to the root level. In contrast, IB2-383 describes moving a sub-page to become a standalone Smart Page (Parent), which directly contradicts the 'no drag-and-drop across Parent' constraint of IB2-215.
suggested_fix: Clarify the scope of page reordering. Either IB2-215 needs to be updated to allow cross-parent drag-and-drop (and potentially merge with IB2-383), or IB2-383 needs to be re-scoped or deferred if cross-parent moves are out of scope for this iteration.

explanation: Story IB2-80 states that if no Master Templates exist, the user 'can select to Create a Blank Template'. Story IB2-337 directly contradicts this by stating that if no Master Templates are available, users 'must not be allowed to create a blank template' and should instead be instructed to create a Master Template first. These two stories define mutually exclusive behaviors for the exact same scenario.
suggested_fix: Product Management needs to clarify the intended behavior when no Master Templates are available. Either users should be allowed to create a blank template (as per IB2-80) or they should be forced to create a Master Template first (as per IB2-337). One of the stories needs to be updated or removed to resolve this logical contradiction.

explanation: Story IB2-463 requires a mechanism to 'complete removal of existing facial data and related flags' during MCP Site handling (implied cloning), while Story IB2-476 explicitly states that 'Existing Unknown face flags must be preserved during clone handling'. These are directly contradictory requirements for the same data during the same operation.
suggested_fix: Clarify the desired behavior for facial data during MCP site cloning. If both options are needed, IB2-463 should be refined to offer this as an *option* (e.g., a checkbox to wipe data), and IB2-476 should specify that *by default* or *if not opted to wipe*, the data is preserved. Otherwise, one story must be updated to align with the other's mandatory behavior.

explanation: Both stories describe the exact same core functionality: repositioning an Image Widget by dragging the image itself. Their descriptions contain nearly identical requirements for visual feedback (bounding boxes, dotted lines) and the behavior of proportional image placement within the block after resizing. If IB2-117 is completed, the effort for IB2-189 would be entirely wasted.
suggested_fix: Consolidate IB2-117 and IB2-189 into a single user story, as they describe the same feature. Ensure all detailed requirements from both are captured in the consolidated story.

explanation: Both stories describe the exact same core functionality: re-ordering pages and sub-pages within the same parent/root using drag-and-drop in the Navigation panel, including the behavior of moving sub-pages with their parent and collapsing them afterwards. Implementing both would result in building the same feature twice.
suggested_fix: Consolidate IB2-385 into IB2-215, or mark one as a duplicate and close it. Ensure all specific requirements from both are captured in the remaining story.

explanation: Both stories have nearly identical titles and descriptions for the core user action of 'creating a sub-page for an existing Smart Page via Nav'. Although IB2-396 has a '(BE)' tag, its description still outlines user-facing steps (clicking a button, seeing a popup) that are covered by IB2-225, which also includes inputting a title and saving. If IB2-225 is implemented as a complete feature, the backend logic for sub-page creation would be included, making IB2-396 redundant as a separate user story for the same action.
suggested_fix: Consolidate IB2-396 into IB2-225. If IB2-396 is intended to be a purely backend task, its title and description should be rephrased to reflect a system-level action or a dependency, rather than a user story for creating a sub-page.

explanation: Both stories have identical titles and descriptions for the core functionality: 'As a User, I want to create a sub-page for an exisiting Smart Page via Nav'. IB2-225 contains all the requirements of IB2-415 and more. Implementing IB2-225 would render IB2-415 completely redundant.
suggested_fix: Consolidate IB2-415 into IB2-225, or mark IB2-415 as a duplicate and close it.

explanation: Story IB2-429 is a near-exact duplicate of the target story IB2-233. Both stories describe the core functionality of seeing the extend/collapse button to open the Left Side Panel as Page Nav and seeing all saved Simple pages within it. Implementing IB2-233 would fully cover the requirements of IB2-429.
suggested_fix: Consolidate IB2-429 into IB2-233. Mark IB2-429 as a duplicate and close it, ensuring any unique details (if any beyond the current description) are merged into IB2-233.

explanation: Both stories describe the exact same 'Bulk Apply' functionality for updates from Grouped work. Their descriptions are nearly identical, covering UI behavior (button counts, tooltips, snackbars), core apply logic, additional validation checks, and the logic for determining 'Complete Request/Stage' actions. Implementing IB2-240 would render IB2-448 completely redundant.
suggested_fix: Consolidate IB2-240 and IB2-448 into a single user story, as they describe the same feature with identical requirements. Choose one story to keep and close the other as a duplicate.

explanation: Both stories define the exact same UI behavior for the 'Ask' button: it is disabled by default when the panel opens (IB2-323 point 1, IB2-326 point 3) and has the same hover text 'Click or hit enter to ask your question.' when enabled (IB2-323 point 2, IB2-326 point 5). Implementing both as written would result in redundant effort for these specific UI details.
suggested_fix: Consolidate the common UI behavior definitions for the 'Ask' button into a single story (e.g., IB2-323) and have other stories (e.g., IB2-326) reference it for these details, focusing IB2-326 on the permission and search-scope specific logic.

explanation: Both stories describe the exact same user interaction flow for creating a sub-page via the navigation panel: 'Be able to click on setting button of a simple page from Nav' and 'Be able to see the sub-page popup'. While IB2-396 specifies 'Simple Page' and IB2-415 specifies 'Smart Page' and adds a step for inputting a title, the core user experience for initiating sub-page creation is duplicated. If IB2-415 is implemented, the UI for steps 1 and 2 of IB2-396 would likely be covered, leading to wasted effort.
suggested_fix: Consolidate these stories into a single user story that covers the creation of sub-pages for both 'Simple Pages' and 'Smart Pages' via the navigation. Alternatively, if IB2-396 is intended to be purely backend, its description should be rephrased to focus solely on backend logic without describing user interface interactions that are covered by IB2-415.

explanation: Story IB2-447 describes a specific UX interaction (hover-to-submit) for a 'Submit & Continue' button within 'Grouped work' bulk submission. Story IB2-448 covers the broader 'Bulk Apply functionality for updates introduced through Grouped work', explicitly mentioning 'Apply button item counts, tooltip text for enabled and disabled button states' and ensuring the experience 'follows the same behavior as the existing Grouped Review page'. It is highly probable that the specific hover behavior and tooltip text for the main submission button in IB2-447 would be covered as part of the comprehensive 'Bulk Apply' implementation in IB2-448.
suggested_fix: Consolidate the specific UX requirement from IB2-447 into IB2-448 as a detailed acceptance criterion for the 'Bulk Apply' functionality. IB2-447 can be closed as a duplicate.

explanation: The story states 'define and manage page display ordering rules' and 'configure the display sequence' but does not specify *any* mechanism or interface for this configuration (e.g., a dedicated settings screen, numerical input fields, or an API for rule definition). This ambiguity makes it difficult for the team to precisely understand the desired behavior and estimate the implementation effort.
suggested_fix: Clarify the intended user interface and interaction for defining and managing page display ordering rules. Provide details on how users will configure the sequence (e.g., a dedicated settings screen, up/down arrows, numerical input fields).

explanation: The description mixes new functionality ('Width slider from Nav') with references to 'existing behaviour' and 'dragging the dot line' for resizing. The precise interaction and expected outcome when using both methods, and how they affect the image's ratio and block's width, are not clearly defined, leading to ambiguity in estimation.
suggested_fix: Clarify the exact interaction model for resizing the Image Widget. Specify how the 'Width slider from Nav' works in conjunction with or independently from 'dragging the dot line,' and ensure the desired behavior for image ratio and block width is explicitly stated.

explanation: The third acceptance criterion, 'Be able to re-position and see the Image in the same percentage of ‘Block’ after resizing to smaller, and the percentage will obtain if the 'Block' width changes by,' is unclear and complex. The phrase 'the percentage will obtain if the 'Block' width changes by' is ambiguous and makes it difficult to precisely understand the desired behavior and estimate the implementation effort.
suggested_fix: Clarify the exact desired behavior for image positioning and scaling relative to its 'Block' when the image is resized and the block width changes. Provide specific examples or visual mockups to illustrate the intended outcome.

explanation: The conditions for who can see the 'assign collaborators' action are complex and potentially ambiguous, especially the second point: 'Other added collaborators who are not one of the above (who are collaborator on the current stage, for staged requests).' It's unclear whether this refers to users who can see the action or users who are the collaborators being assigned, leading to uncertainty in scoping the visibility logic.
suggested_fix: Clarify the precise visibility rules for the 'assign collaborators' action. Explicitly state which user roles and conditions allow a user to see and perform this action, especially clarifying the intent of the second point.

explanation: Point 4 ('Support historical data migration, multi-environment aggregation, backward compatibility, and automated export/report synchronization across reporting systems') introduces an extremely broad and undefined scope. The technical complexity and effort required for each of these sub-items are immense and highly ambiguous, making it impossible to estimate with any reasonable confidence.
suggested_fix: Decompose point 4 into separate, well-defined stories or epics. Each new story should clearly define its scope, requirements, and technical approach to allow for proper estimation.

explanation: The story title ('As a User, I want new Auth Screens + Success Page - Add in Menu / Login Bar') directly contradicts the description ('The tiger team consensus is to show a logo, but not the log-in or nav menu'). This fundamental disagreement or lack of clarity on the desired outcome makes it impossible to understand the scope or estimate the work required.
suggested_fix: Resolve the contradiction between the story title and description. Clarify the exact desired behavior for the new Auth Screens and Success Page, specifically regarding the presence or absence of the menu/login bar.

explanation: The first acceptance criterion, 'Be able to select ‘Session' together with ‘Hourly’, ‘Daily’, and 'Weekly’ at the same time,' is ambiguous. It's unclear whether this means a user can choose multiple digest frequencies simultaneously (e.g., both 'Session' and 'Daily'), and if so, how these selections would interact (e.g., would they receive multiple emails or a combined digest?). This lack of clarity makes it difficult to estimate the implementation logic.
suggested_fix: Clarify the intended interaction for selecting digest email frequencies. Specify whether 'Session' is an exclusive option or if it can be combined with other frequencies, and if so, define the expected behavior when multiple frequencies are selected.

explanation: The description is vague regarding the placement ('in the Restrictions tab in MCP or Custom Page') and display ('Do it as it should be shown somewhere on Custom Page'), making it difficult to estimate the scope and effort.
suggested_fix: Specify the exact location for the 'Drag & Drop limit' setting and provide clear design specifications for how it should be displayed on the Custom Page.

explanation: The story is incomplete and vague. It mentions 'configure the new widget settings' and 'for each widgets:' without specifying which widgets or what 'new widget settings' are to be configured, making the scope undefined.
suggested_fix: Clearly list the specific widgets and the exact properties that need to be configurable. Provide design details for the configuration interface.

explanation: The story states 'see detailed locations to rename the keyword as below: Location to rename' but does not provide the actual list of locations within the story. Without this list, the scope of the renaming task is unknown, making it impossible to estimate.
suggested_fix: Include the comprehensive list of all front-end locations where 'Simple Page' needs to be renamed to 'Smart Page' directly in the story description or link to a definitive document containing this list.

explanation: The story lacks clarity on the core 'Actual apply logic' and mentions 'Behaviour here need extra check', indicating significant ambiguity and undefined requirements for the central functionality. This makes it impossible to estimate with confidence.
suggested_fix: Define the 'Actual apply logic' in detail, specifying the exact steps and conditions for bulk application. Clarify what 'extra check' is needed and incorporate it into the requirements.

explanation: The phrase "link the status" is vague and does not specify the desired behavior or implications when a background image's status changes in the Resource Module. This lack of clarity makes it difficult to estimate the implementation effort.
suggested_fix: Clarify the exact behavior expected when the status of a background image resource changes. For example, should the image disappear, show a placeholder, or display a specific status indicator in the Page Builder?

explanation: The story describes a complex scenario involving 'Cross Folder bulk edit' and 'different reviewers for different folders' but fails to specify the desired user action, outcome, or expected system behavior. It is a description of a problem space or context, not a clear, actionable user story, making it impossible to estimate.
suggested_fix: Rephrase this as a clear user story by defining WHO is doing WHAT and WHY. Specify the desired functionality, user interaction, and expected system response in this complex scenario.

explanation: The story states 'set Page limits' but does not specify what kind of limits (e.g., number of pages, page size, content types, widget count) or the parameters involved. This ambiguity makes it difficult to understand the scope and estimate the implementation effort for the settings UI and underlying logic.
suggested_fix: Clarify the specific 'Page limits' that an internal user should be able to set. Define the types of limits, their configurable parameters, and any associated validation rules.

explanation: The third point of the description ('Support hierarchical permission inheritance, multi-level propagation, approval workflows, audit history, and cross-platform synchronization for all sub-page permission updates') introduces an extremely broad and complex scope that would be impossible to estimate with reasonable confidence in a single story.
suggested_fix: Break down the story into multiple smaller, more focused stories. Each aspect mentioned in point 3 (e.g., hierarchical inheritance, approval workflows, audit history, cross-platform synchronization) should be evaluated as a separate, potentially epic-level, requirement.

explanation: The first point mentions 'additional (or could be unknown) Notification options may have some chances to appear,' which is highly ambiguous. The second point lists a vast array of complex features (global template inheritance, multilingual email generation, real-time delivery tracking, etc.) that would be impossible to estimate as a single, coherent story due to undefined scope and technical complexity.
suggested_fix: Clarify the exact notification options and their behavior in point 1. Break down the extensive requirements in point 2 into separate, well-defined user stories or an epic, each with clear scope and acceptance criteria.

explanation: The description is vague regarding 'new fields' and only explicitly mentions 'Response Comment.' It's unclear if other fields are implied or if the story is solely about ensuring existing logic applies to this one field, making the scope ambiguous and difficult to estimate.
suggested_fix: Clearly list all 'new fields' that should be displayed and specify the exact conditions under which they appear. If it's only 'Response Comment,' rephrase the title and description to reflect that specific scope.

explanation: While the UI (a checkbox) seems simple, the backend implications of 'complete removal of existing facial data and related flags' could be highly complex, involving database operations, data integrity, performance considerations for large datasets, and potential dependencies. The description lacks sufficient detail to confidently estimate the technical effort.
suggested_fix: Provide more technical detail on what 'complete removal' entails (e.g., database tables affected, performance requirements, rollback strategy) or break down the backend work into a separate technical task if the UI is to be done first.

explanation: The description uses ambiguous language such as 'should probably see', 'may show', 'may be hidden', and 'may autoplay'. This lack of clear, definitive requirements for the preview's behavior (e.g., controls, autoplay) makes it difficult for the team to accurately estimate the effort.
suggested_fix: Clarify the exact expected behavior for the GIF/Lottie MP4 preview on the Share screen. Specifically, define whether controls should be visible/hidden, if it should autoplay, and any other interaction details.

explanation: The story introduces a significant permission bypass mechanism ('even if they lack permission to the source item module, etc') which could have broad security implications and edge cases that are not fully defined, making it difficult to estimate the full scope and effort.
suggested_fix: Clarify the exact scope and implications of the 'bypass permissions' for collaborators. Define specific scenarios and expected behavior when a collaborator lacks module permissions but is granted access via this setting.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-215 'PB - As a User, I want to re-order pages within the same parent/root via Nav - FE integration' explicitly relies on the underlying 'page display ordering rules' defined in IB2-108. It also depends on the 'Page Category' setting from IB2-109 and the 'Nav panel' from IB2-233 for its UI. Without IB2-108 defining the ordering rules, IB2-215 cannot implement the drag-and-drop reordering functionality.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-108 to define the page ordering rules. Also ensure IB2-109 (for Page Category setting) and IB2-233 (for Nav panel) are completed as prerequisites for the UI integration.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-383 'PB - As a User, I want to move a sub page to become a standalone Smart Page (Parent)' relies on the 'Drag & Drop Pages - Page Category' MCP setting from IB2-109, the 'Nav panel' from IB2-233, the existence of 'sub-pages' (created by IB2-225), and the underlying 'page display ordering rules' from IB2-108. Without these foundational elements, the complex functionality of moving a sub-page and updating its hierarchy, permissions, and URLs cannot be implemented or tested.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-109 for the 'Page Category' setting, IB2-233 for the Nav panel, IB2-225 for sub-page creation, and IB2-108 for page ordering rules before implementing the advanced sub-page movement functionality in IB2-383.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-385 'As a User, I want to rearrange pages under the same parent/root through the Navigation panel UI' explicitly relies on the underlying 'page display ordering rules' defined in IB2-108. It also depends on the 'Page Category' setting from IB2-109 and the 'Nav panel' from IB2-233 for its UI. Without IB2-108 defining the ordering rules, IB2-385 cannot implement the drag-and-drop reordering functionality.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-108 to define the page ordering rules. Also ensure IB2-109 (for Page Category setting) and IB2-233 (for Nav panel) are completed as prerequisites for the UI integration.

explanation: [EXTREME_BOTTLENECK] Story IB2-108 'As a User, I want to define and manage page display ordering rules for All Pages and Page Categories' defines the foundational rules for page ordering. Several other stories depend on these rules for implementing drag-and-drop reordering or other hierarchy management, making IB2-108 a bottleneck. It has 2 strong blocking dependents and 1 moderate blocking dependent.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-108 to establish the core page ordering rules before implementing UI interactions that rely on these rules. Ensure the backend logic for ordering is robust.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-111 'As a User, I want to have previous 'Block' background colour setting embedded in Text Widget toolbar after combining these two' directly references the 'Widgets combining Block' concept, which is introduced and controlled by an MCP setting in IB2-109. Without IB2-109 establishing this foundational concept and its enabling setting, IB2-111 cannot be implemented or tested.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-109 to establish the 'Widgets combining Block' MCP setting before implementing UI changes that rely on this combined widget behavior.

explanation: [EXTREME_BOTTLENECK] Story IB2-109 'As an Internal User, I want to have MCP(development use only) settings to enable/disable features per Epic for Drag & Drop Page MVP' is a foundational story that introduces multiple MCP settings for Drag & Drop Pages. Many other stories directly depend on these settings for their core functionality, making IB2-109 a significant bottleneck. It has 18 strong blocking dependents and 3 moderate blocking dependents.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-109 to be completed early in the development cycle to unblock a large number of dependent stories. Consider breaking down IB2-109 if possible, or ensuring its scope is well-defined and stable.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-190 'As a User, I want the position and size that I set of the image to still work in different responsive modes' relies on the 'Drag & Drop Pages: Image Resizing' feature, which is controlled by an MCP setting in IB2-109. It also explicitly references the resizing and repositioning actions defined in stories like IB2-116 and IB2-117. Without IB2-109 enabling the core image resizing feature and the specific resizing/repositioning behaviors, IB2-190 cannot be fully implemented or tested.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-109 for the foundational 'Image Resizing' setting, and ensure IB2-116 and IB2-117 (or their intended replacements) are completed to provide the resizing/repositioning functionality that IB2-190 makes responsive.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-191 'As a User, I want to Undo & Redo resizing & repositioning' relies on the 'Drag & Drop Pages: Image Resizing' feature, which is controlled by an MCP setting in IB2-109. It also explicitly references the resizing and repositioning actions defined in stories like IB2-116 and IB2-117. Without IB2-109 enabling the core image resizing feature and the specific resizing/repositioning behaviors, IB2-191 cannot be fully implemented or tested.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-109 for the foundational 'Image Resizing' setting, and ensure IB2-116 and IB2-117 (or their intended replacements) are completed to provide the resizing/repositioning functionality that IB2-191 provides undo/redo for.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-116 'As a User, I want to resize Image Widget by Width slider from Nav' relies on the 'Drag & Drop Pages: Image Resizing' feature, which is controlled by an MCP setting in IB2-109. Furthermore, IB2-308 directly contradicts IB2-116 by stating 'The Image Widget's width must not be changeable from the Nav Panel Width slider' and 'Users must resize the Image Widget only by dragging the dotted outline directly on the page.' This means IB2-116 cannot be delivered as described if IB2-308 is also implemented.
suggested_fix: Resolve the conflict between IB2-116 and IB2-308 regarding image resizing behavior. Determine the intended final behavior and update one or both stories accordingly. Prioritize IB2-109 for the foundational 'Image Resizing' setting.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-117 'As a User, I want to reposition Image Widget by dragging image itself when create/editing a Page' relies on the 'Drag & Drop Pages: Image Resizing' feature, which is controlled by an MCP setting in IB2-109. Without IB2-109 establishing this foundational setting, IB2-117 cannot be implemented or tested.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-109 to establish the 'Image Resizing' MCP setting before implementing UI interactions that rely on this feature.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-164 'As an internal User, I'd love to set Drag & Drop Page limit via Custom Page limit in MCP' relies on the 'Drag & Drop Pages: Page Templates' feature, which is controlled by an MCP setting in IB2-109. Furthermore, IB2-389 directly contradicts IB2-164 by stating 'Be able to set Page limits on MCP control panel for client sites' and explicitly 'not in Custom Page'. This means IB2-164 cannot be delivered as described if IB2-389 is also implemented.
suggested_fix: Resolve the conflict between IB2-164 and IB2-389 regarding the location for setting Drag & Drop Page limits. Determine the intended final location and update one or both stories accordingly. Prioritize IB2-109 for the foundational 'Page Templates' setting.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-186 'PB - As a User, I want to configure both Block and Text Widget properties' directly references the 'Widgets combining Block' concept, which is introduced and controlled by an MCP setting in IB2-109. Without IB2-109 establishing this foundational concept and its enabling setting, IB2-186 cannot be implemented or tested.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-109 to establish the 'Widgets combining Block' MCP setting before implementing UI for configuring combined widget properties.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-189 'As a User, I want to drag the image directly to adjust the position of an Image Widget while creating or editing a Page' relies on the 'Drag & Drop Pages: Image Resizing' feature, which is controlled by an MCP setting in IB2-109. Without IB2-109 establishing this foundational setting, IB2-189 cannot be implemented or tested.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-109 to establish the 'Image Resizing' MCP setting before implementing UI interactions that rely on this feature.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-208 'PB - As a User, I want to only select & see the combo of Widget and Block (bounding box experience) in Page Builder' directly references the 'Widgets combining Block' concept, which is introduced and controlled by an MCP setting in IB2-109. Without IB2-109 establishing this foundational concept and its enabling setting, IB2-208 cannot be implemented or tested.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-109 to establish the 'Widgets combining Block' MCP setting before implementing UI selection and bounding box experiences for combined widgets.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-225 'PB - As a User, I want to create a sub-page for an exisiting Smart Page via Nav' relies on the 'Drag & Drop Pages - Page Category' MCP setting from IB2-109 and the 'Nav panel' from IB2-233 for its UI. Without IB2-109 enabling the Page Category feature and IB2-233 providing the Nav panel, IB2-225 cannot be implemented or tested. (Note: IB2-396 and IB2-415 are duplicates of this story, reinforcing the dependency).
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-109 to enable the 'Page Category' MCP setting and IB2-233 to establish the Nav panel before implementing sub-page creation via the Nav panel.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-394 'PB - As a User, I want to see sub-pages in Custom Page v2 index list (with a subtitle for parent)' requires the ability to list Smart Pages (via IB2-51), the existence of sub-pages (created by IB2-225), and the 'Page Category' setting from IB2-109 to correctly display the hierarchy. The core acceptance criteria of seeing sub-pages with parent subtitles cannot be validated without these components.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-51 for listing Smart Pages, IB2-225 for creating sub-pages, and IB2-109 for the 'Page Category' setting before implementing the display of sub-pages in the v2 index list.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-395 'PB - As a User, I want to configure permissions of sub-pages in Admin -> Group' relies on the 'Drag & Drop Pages - Page Category' MCP setting from IB2-109 and the existence of 'sub-pages' (created by IB2-225). Without IB2-109 enabling the Page Category feature and IB2-225 providing the ability to create sub-pages, the configuration of sub-page permissions cannot be implemented or tested.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-109 to enable the 'Page Category' MCP setting and IB2-225 to implement sub-page creation before implementing sub-page permission configuration.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-233 'PB - As a User, I want to see Nav panel when viewing a Simple Page' relies on the 'Drag & Drop Pages - Page Category' MCP setting from IB2-109 and the ability to 'Get a list of Smart Pages via API' from IB2-51 to populate the navigation. Without IB2-109 enabling the Page Category feature and IB2-51 providing the list of pages, the Nav panel cannot be fully implemented or tested. (Note: IB2-429 is a duplicate of this story, reinforcing the dependency).
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-109 to enable the 'Page Category' MCP setting and IB2-51 to provide the API for listing Smart Pages before implementing the Nav panel UI.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-286 'PB - As a User, I want to edit (rename) an existing Page Category in Page Builder' relies on the 'Drag & Drop Pages - Page Category' MCP setting from IB2-109. Without IB2-109 enabling the Page Category feature, IB2-286 cannot be implemented or tested.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-109 to enable the 'Page Category' MCP setting before implementing the ability to edit page categories.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-305 'PB - As a User, I want to configure both Block and Button Widget properties' directly references the 'Widgets combining Block' concept, which is introduced and controlled by an MCP setting in IB2-109. Without IB2-109 establishing this foundational concept and its enabling setting, IB2-305 cannot be implemented or tested.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-109 to establish the 'Widgets combining Block' MCP setting before implementing UI for configuring combined widget properties.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-308 'As a User, I want Image Widget resizing to be controlled only by direct dragging, not by the Width slider from Nav' relies on the 'Drag & Drop Pages: Image Resizing' feature, which is controlled by an MCP setting in IB2-109. Without IB2-109 establishing this foundational setting, IB2-308 cannot be implemented or tested.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-109 to establish the 'Image Resizing' MCP setting before implementing UI interactions that rely on this feature.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-337 'PB - As a User, I want template selection to require an existing Master Template before creating a Smart Page' relies on the 'Drag & Drop Pages: Page Templates' feature, which is controlled by an MCP setting in IB2-109. Without IB2-109 establishing this foundational setting, IB2-337 cannot be implemented or tested.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-109 to establish the 'Page Templates' MCP setting before implementing template selection logic.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-347 'PB - As a User, I want to add Widgets directly to a Section' directly references the 'Widgets combining Block' concept, which is introduced and controlled by an MCP setting in IB2-109. Without IB2-109 establishing this foundational concept and its enabling setting, IB2-347 cannot be implemented or tested.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-109 to establish the 'Widgets combining Block' MCP setting before implementing the ability to drag widgets directly to a section.

explanation: [EXTREME_BOTTLENECK] Story IB2-75 'As a User, I want MCP Setting for Approvals List Bulk Actions Updates' is a foundational story that introduces an MCP setting for V3 Approvals List Bulk Actions. Many other stories directly depend on this setting for their core functionality, making IB2-75 a significant bottleneck. It has 8 strong blocking dependents.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-75 to be completed early in the development cycle to unblock a large number of dependent stories. Ensure its scope is well-defined and stable.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-120 'As a user, I want to see an action to assign collaborators in action column in V3 Approval List' explicitly states 'When setting is enabled for the Workflow Template, a NEW action will display in Approvals List'. This setting is introduced by IB2-75 'MCP Setting for Approvals List Bulk Actions Updates'. Additionally, the logic for 'who I can select as collaborator' is defined in IB2-94. Without IB2-75 enabling the bulk actions context and IB2-94 defining the collaborator selection rules, IB2-120 cannot be fully implemented or tested.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-75 to enable the bulk actions setting and IB2-94 to define collaborator selection logic before implementing the UI action in IB2-120.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-78 'As a User, when I create, update and delete a Server and the action has failed, show a snackbar message to show the error' states that 'After deleting a Server and it fails, the dialog remains open(?) and a snackbar appears with the error message'. This directly contradicts IB2-172, which explicitly states 'When deletion fails, the confirmation dialog must close automatically' and 'No snackbar error message should be displayed after a failed delete action'. If IB2-172 is implemented, IB2-78's behavior for delete failures cannot be delivered as described.
suggested_fix: Resolve the conflict between IB2-78 and IB2-172 regarding the behavior of failed server deletions. Determine the intended final behavior and update one or both stories accordingly.

explanation: [EXTREME_BOTTLENECK] Story IB2-225 'PB - (DND replace) As a User, I want to create a sub-page for an exisiting Smart Page via Nav' (and its duplicates IB2-396, IB2-415) provides the ability to create sub-pages. Several other stories depend on the existence of these sub-pages for their functionality, making IB2-225 a bottleneck. It has 3 moderate blocking dependents.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-225 to enable sub-page creation, as it is a prerequisite for managing and displaying sub-page hierarchies. Consolidate duplicates (IB2-396, IB2-415) into this story.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-240 'As a user I can Bulk Apply (updates from Grouped work)' explicitly requires the MCP development setting from IB2-75 'Approvals - Request List (V3) - Bulk Action Updates' to be enabled, and the 'new column for the Bulk Actions Checkboxes' from IB2-245 to display. Without both of these prerequisites, the core functionality of bulk applying updates cannot be implemented or tested. (Note: IB2-448 is a duplicate of this story, reinforcing the dependency).
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-75 to enable the bulk actions MCP setting and IB2-245 to implement the checkbox column before implementing the Bulk Apply functionality in IB2-240.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-455 'As a user when one checkbox of request is checked only the requests that can be bulk approved can be selected' describes the conditional selection logic for checkboxes in a bulk approval context. This functionality relies on the MCP setting from IB2-75 'Approvals - Request List (V3) - Bulk Action Updates' being enabled, and the 'new column for the Bulk Actions Checkboxes' from IB2-245 to display. Without both of these prerequisites, the core selection logic cannot be implemented or tested.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-75 to enable the bulk actions MCP setting and IB2-245 to implement the checkbox column before implementing the conditional selection logic in IB2-455.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-245 'As a user I only see the checkbox *column* when relevant (core mcp / template level check)' explicitly states that the checkbox column will display 'when: MCP development setting from is enabled' and 'at least one active Template has the new setting enabled'. This MCP setting is introduced by IB2-75 'MCP Setting for Approvals List Bulk Actions Updates'. Without IB2-75 establishing this setting, IB2-245 cannot be implemented or tested.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-75 to establish the MCP setting for Bulk Action Updates before implementing the conditional display of the checkbox column in IB2-245.

explanation: [EXTREME_BOTTLENECK] Story IB2-52 'As a User, I want to Get a Smart page, including details: Page, Sections, Blocks, and Widgets via API' provides the API for retrieving detailed Smart Page information. Several other stories depend on this API to display or manipulate content within Smart Pages, making IB2-52 a bottleneck. It has 4 moderate blocking dependents.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-52 to ensure the Smart Page detail API is stable and available for dependent stories. Consider implementing it with robust mock data if UI development needs to proceed in parallel.

explanation: [EXTREME_BOTTLENECK] Story IB2-465 'PB - As a User, I want to see and access resource module via a lookup button to select & upload Resources into Page Builder' provides the core functionality for adding resources to the Page Builder. Several other stories depend on this lookup mechanism to integrate resources into pages, making IB2-465 a bottleneck. It has 3 moderate blocking dependents.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-465 to implement the Resource Lookup button and its functionality, as it is a prerequisite for integrating various resource types into the Page Builder.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-281 'PB - As a User, I want the status of background Image added via Resource Lookup to be linked with Resource module' requires the ability to add a background image via 'Resource Lookup' (provided by IB2-465) and for that image to be part of a 'Page' or 'Section' in Page Builder (which implies the page structure is retrievable via IB2-52). The core acceptance criteria of linking the status cannot be validated without both the mechanism to add the resource and the page structure to contain it.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-465 to implement the Resource Lookup button and IB2-52 to provide the API for retrieving Smart Page details before implementing the status linking for background images.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-326 'As a Main Admin user, I can click the Ask button to trigger AI search after permission and search-scope controls are completed' explicitly states dependencies on IB2-318 (Alpha Main Admin access restriction) and IB2-299 ('Limit to current view' search-scope logic). It also describes the behavior of the 'Ask' button, which is introduced in IB2-323. Without these foundational components, IB2-326 cannot be implemented or tested.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-318 for Main Admin access, IB2-299 for search-scope logic, and IB2-323 for the 'Ask' button UI before implementing the complex triggering logic in IB2-326.

explanation: [EXTREME_BOTTLENECK] Story IB2-318 '[Ask AI Alpha] As a Main Admin user, only I and other Main Admins can use 'Ask AI' in Alpha phase' is a foundational story that gates access to the 'Ask AI Alpha' feature. Several other stories depend on this access control for their functionality, making IB2-318 a bottleneck. It has 2 strong blocking dependents and 1 moderate blocking dependent.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-318 to establish the core access control for the 'Ask AI Alpha' feature, as it is a prerequisite for all related functionalities. Ensure the permission logic is robust.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-299 'As a user I want to choose to search whole platform or current location (Logic: Filter/Search results limit)' introduces the 'Limit to current view' checkbox. Story IB2-326 explicitly states 'The Ask button must use the “Limit to current view” checkbox state to decide whether to search the current location or the whole platform'. This means IB2-299 provides a critical input for IB2-326's core functionality, making it a strong blocking dependency. Additionally, it's part of the 'Ask AI Alpha' feature, which is gated by IB2-318.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-318 to enable the 'Ask AI Alpha' feature and IB2-299 to implement the 'Limit to current view' checkbox before implementing the Ask button logic in IB2-326.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-9 'As a user, I want to be able to preview the GIF or Lottie File converted MP4 on the Share screen' explicitly falls under the scope of 'GIF/Lottie Preview tickets' mentioned in IB2-3. Story IB2-3 states that if its MCP setting 'Resources - GIF/Lottie Preview Support' is disabled, 'no GIF/Lottie preview features should be available on the platform.' Therefore, IB2-9 cannot be implemented, tested, or delivered independently without IB2-3 providing the foundational feature flag and enabling the functionality.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-3 to establish the feature flag and its control logic before implementing IB2-9.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-323 'As a user I can click 'Ask' button to trigger search AI' describes the 'Ask' button. Its visibility is controlled by IB2-318 ('only Main Admin users can see the ‘AI Assist’ button'), and its enabling logic and search trigger are explicitly defined in IB2-326 ('The Ask button must only be displayed after the Alpha Main Admin access restriction has been implemented' and 'Once the user enters a valid question, the Ask button becomes enabled'). Therefore, IB2-323 cannot be implemented, tested, or delivered independently without both IB2-318 and IB2-326 being completed.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-318 to implement the Main Admin access restriction and IB2-326 to define the Ask button's display and enabling logic before implementing the button itself in IB2-323.

explanation: [CIRCULAR_DEPENDENCY] Story IB2-323 describes the basic UI and initial state of the 'Ask' button. Story IB2-326 defines the complex display and enabling logic for this same 'Ask' button, explicitly stating it 'must only be displayed after the Alpha Main Admin access restriction has been implemented' and 'Once the user enters a valid question, the Ask button becomes enabled'. This creates a circular dependency where IB2-323 provides the button that IB2-326 controls, and IB2-326 defines the conditions for IB2-323's button to be functional and visible. Neither can be fully implemented or tested without the other.
suggested_fix: Refactor these stories. IB2-323 should focus solely on the button's visual presence and initial disabled state. IB2-326 should then implement the logic for enabling/disabling and triggering the search, assuming the button from IB2-323 exists. Alternatively, combine them if they are too tightly coupled.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-80 'As a user who has selected Master Template option, if I can see none, I am directed to Create a blank one instead' directly contradicts IB2-337, which states 'If no Master Templates are available, users must not be allowed to create a blank template. Instead, display a message instructing users to create a Master Template before continuing.' This direct conflict means IB2-80 cannot be delivered as described if IB2-337 is also implemented.
suggested_fix: Clarify the intended behavior for creating blank templates when no master templates exist. Either IB2-80 needs to be updated to align with IB2-337's more restrictive approach, or IB2-337 needs to be re-evaluated.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-366 'Guest Upload MVP: Success message should randomly change its confirmation style after submission' explicitly states that 'This success state should reuse the same public-user page header layout where the Database name and close icon have already been removed (should wait until IB-382 completed)' and 'The randomized confirmation presentation should only be applied after the public upload page uses the simplified header state for Public users'. This makes IB2-382 a direct and explicit blocking dependency for IB2-366.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-382 to implement the simplified public-user page header before implementing the randomized success message styling in IB2-366.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-387 'PB - As a User, I want to be able to download the resources added to the page, if I have permission to do so - Image widget' requires the ability to retrieve Smart Page details (via IB2-52) to display the Image Widget and explicitly depends on the permission logic defined in IB2-388 ('if I have permission to do so'). The core acceptance criteria of conditionally displaying the download button based on permissions cannot be validated without IB2-388 being in place.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-52 to provide the API for retrieving Smart Page details and IB2-388 to implement the resource permission lost scenario before implementing the conditional download button in IB2-387.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-388 'PB - As a User, I want the status of Resource added via Resource Lookup to be linked with Resource module - resource permission lost scenario' requires the ability to add resources via 'Resource Lookup' (provided by IB2-465) and for those resources to be part of a Smart Page (which implies the page structure is retrievable via IB2-52). The core acceptance criteria of displaying placeholders/permission lost icons cannot be validated without both the mechanism to add the resource and the page structure to contain it.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-465 to implement the Resource Lookup button and IB2-52 to provide the API for retrieving Smart Page details before implementing the permission lost scenario for resources.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-413 'Grouped Resource Publish - Grouped Email Notification Settings added to Resource Publish Templates' explicitly states 'When MCP development setting is enabled...'. This MCP setting is introduced by IB2-75 'MCP Setting for Approvals List Bulk Actions Updates'. Without IB2-75 establishing this setting, IB2-413 cannot be implemented or tested.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-75 to establish the MCP setting for Bulk Action Updates before implementing grouped email notification settings.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-420 '[RESTRICTIONS] Cross param handling of 'Hide Decline' (hide Decline if all share param, ignore Bulk Apply of Decline to items that don't apply)' describes behavior for the 'Bulk Review feature'. This feature's context is enabled by the MCP setting introduced in IB2-75 'MCP Setting for Approvals List Bulk Actions Updates'. Without IB2-75 establishing this setting, IB2-420 cannot be implemented or tested.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-75 to establish the MCP setting for Bulk Action Updates before implementing cross-parameter handling for 'Hide Decline' in bulk review.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-436 'BP - As a User, I want the single selected Video resource via Resource Lookup to appear in Video Widget (preview linkage)' requires the ability to add a video resource via 'Resource Lookup' (provided by IB2-465) and for that video to be part of a 'Video Widget' on a page (which implies the page structure is retrievable via IB2-52). The core acceptance criteria of displaying the selected video cannot be validated without both the mechanism to add the resource and the page structure to contain it.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-465 to implement the Resource Lookup button and IB2-52 to provide the API for retrieving Smart Page details before implementing video widget preview linkage.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-447 'As a user I want Bulk Submit to require me to hover over the Submit & Continue button before I can submit updates from Grouped work' describes behavior for 'Bulk Submit' in the context of 'Grouped work'. This functionality is enabled by the MCP setting introduced in IB2-75 'MCP Setting for Approvals List Bulk Actions Updates'. Without IB2-75 establishing this setting, IB2-447 cannot be implemented or tested.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-75 to establish the MCP setting for Bulk Action Updates before implementing the hover requirement for Bulk Submit.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-450 'As a user I can open and view the Bulk Approve Review Page (Core UI)' describes the opening of the 'Bulk Review page'. This page's context is enabled by the MCP setting introduced in IB2-75 'MCP Setting for Approvals List Bulk Actions Updates'. Without IB2-75 establishing this setting, IB2-450 cannot be implemented or tested.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-75 to establish the MCP setting for Bulk Action Updates before implementing the Bulk Approve Review Page.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] Story IB2-476 'As a User, I want Unknown People filters to remain available after cloning an MCP Site' explicitly states 'After cloning an MCP Site...' and 'Existing Unknown face flags must be preserved during clone handling'. This directly depends on the 'Clone MCP Site handling' functionality provided by IB2-463. Without IB2-463 implementing the cloning process, IB2-476 cannot be implemented or tested.
suggested_fix: Prioritize IB2-463 to implement the MCP site cloning functionality before implementing the preservation of Unknown People filters during cloning in IB2-476.

explanation: This story bundles 10 distinct and significant tasks, including creating multiple settings, migrating data, updating billing, adding analytics, notifications, and updating APIs/documentation. This scope is too broad to be completed within a single sprint and should be split into several smaller, independently valuable stories.
suggested_fix: Break this story down into multiple smaller user stories, each focusing on a single, cohesive piece of functionality (e.g., 'Enable Azure Image Tagging Setting', 'Migrate Imagga Data to Azure', 'Implement Azure Image Tagging Billing & Quota', 'Add Azure Image Tagging Analytics & Notifications').

explanation: The story includes a massive scope in point 4 ('Support historical data migration, multi-environment aggregation, backward compatibility, and automated export/report synchronization across reporting systems'). Each of these sub-items represents a significant, complex undertaking that could easily be an epic or multiple stories on its own, making the entire story far too large for a single sprint.
suggested_fix: Break down point 4 into multiple, smaller, independent user stories or epics. Each aspect (historical data migration, multi-environment aggregation, backward compatibility, automated export/synchronization) should be addressed as a separate, manageable unit of work.

explanation: The story bundles multiple distinct re-ordering scenarios: re-ordering root-pages, re-ordering sub-pages within the same parent, and moving sub-pages along with their parent. While related, these are distinct user goals and implementation complexities that could be split.
suggested_fix: Split this story into smaller, independent stories. For example: 'As a User, I want to re-order root-level pages via drag-and-drop in the Nav Panel' and 'As a User, I want to re-order sub-pages within the same parent via drag-and-drop in the Nav Panel'.

explanation: The story title mentions configuring properties for 'both Block and Button Widget properties', but the description only details properties for the 'Button' widget. 'Block' properties in a page builder context can be extensive (e.g., layout, padding, background, responsiveness), making the scope for 'Block' potentially too broad for a single sprint-sized story.
suggested_fix: Split this story into two or more smaller stories, one for 'Button' widget properties and another for 'Block' widget properties, with clear definitions for each. Alternatively, explicitly define the specific 'Block' properties to be configured if the scope is intended to be limited.

explanation: Implementing robust drag-and-drop functionality for widgets from a navigation panel into specific sections of a page builder, including visual feedback, drop zone validation, and state persistence, is typically a complex front-end task that can easily exceed the scope of a single sprint-sized story.
suggested_fix: Break down the drag-and-drop functionality into smaller, more manageable stories. For example, one story for basic drag-and-drop, another for visual feedback, and subsequent stories for advanced placement rules or error handling.

explanation: This story encompasses an enormous scope, including complex data migration (child references, linked content, permissions, dependencies), updates to URLs/SEO, undo/redo functionality, full page hierarchy version history, audit logging, notifications, workflow integration, role-based permissions, and synchronization across multiple clients/APIs. Each of these points could be a significant story or even an epic on its own, making this story impossible to complete within a single sprint.
suggested_fix: Break this epic-level story down into multiple, smaller, independently valuable user stories. Prioritize the core 'move sub-page to standalone' functionality first, then address the complex cross-cutting concerns (e.g., permissions, versioning, synchronization) in subsequent stories.

explanation: The story's scope, particularly point 3, encompasses multiple distinct and complex functionalities (hierarchical permission inheritance, multi-level propagation, approval workflows, audit history, cross-platform synchronization). This is far too large for a single sprint and should be broken down into an epic or multiple smaller stories.
suggested_fix: Split this into an epic, then define smaller, independent user stories for each major piece of functionality (e.g., 'As an Admin, I want to configure hierarchical permission inheritance for sub-pages,' 'As an Admin, I want sub-page permission updates to be included in audit history').

explanation: The second point of the description ('Requester Emails notification settings should support global template inheritance, multilingual email generation, tenant-level customization, real-time delivery tracking, approval workflows, and synchronization with external notification providers and legacy workflow systems') describes an epic-level amount of work, bundling numerous complex and distinct functionalities.
suggested_fix: Split this into an epic, then define smaller, independent user stories for each major piece of functionality (e.g., 'As an Admin, I want to configure multilingual email generation for requester notifications,' 'As an Admin, I want to track real-time delivery of requester emails').

explanation: The story bundles several distinct and potentially complex functionalities: ensuring UI behavior consistency (item counts, tooltips, snackbars), supporting existing processing logic, adding *new* validation checks, and determining complex 'Complete Request' and 'Stage' actions based on various request types and statuses. This scope is too broad for a single sprint.
suggested_fix: Split the story into smaller, independent user stories. For example, one story for implementing the core 'Bulk Apply' processing logic, another for ensuring UI consistency, and separate stories for specific 'additional validation checks' or the complex 'Determine Complete Request and Stage actions' logic.

explanation: The described behavior of the Page Category name briefly disappearing and reappearing, and the navigation reloading every time a page is opened, creates an undesirable and potentially confusing user experience, actively detracting from usability rather than adding value.
suggested_fix: Re-evaluate the user's actual need. This sounds like a workaround for an underlying UI issue or a misunderstanding of desired behavior. The story should be rephrased to describe a positive, stable user experience.

explanation: This story is phrased as a user's doubt or question ('I'm doubting about what both Block and Button Widget properties are for') rather than a clear, actionable software requirement or a description of a feature to be built. It indicates a need for clarification, documentation, or a UI/UX improvement, but it is not a user story that can be implemented directly.
suggested_fix: Rephrase this as a proper user story describing a desired outcome (e.g., 'As a User, I want clear explanations for Block and Button Widget properties so I can understand their purpose and use them effectively') or convert it into a task for documentation/UX review.

explanation: The story states a desire to 'set Drag & Drop Page limit' but fails to explain the 'why' or the tangible business/user benefit. Without understanding the problem it solves (e.g., performance, control, preventing misuse), its value is unclear.
suggested_fix: Clarify the business outcome or user problem this limit addresses. For example, 'to prevent performance degradation when pages exceed X elements' or 'to ensure consistent page load times for end-users'.

explanation: The story describes a behavior ('briefly disappear and reappear') that actively harms the user experience without any stated benefit. This sounds like a workaround for a technical issue or a misunderstanding of a design, rather than a valuable user feature.
suggested_fix: Re-evaluate the necessity of this behavior. If it's a technical workaround, address the underlying technical issue. If it's a design requirement, clarify the user benefit or interaction goal it aims to achieve.

explanation: Introducing random changes to the success message's style, icon size, wording position, and presentation creates an inconsistent and unpredictable user experience. Users generally prefer consistent feedback, and this randomness actively harms usability and user trust rather than providing tangible value.
suggested_fix: Re-evaluate the design choice. A consistent and clear success message is generally preferred. If variety is desired, consider a limited set of predefined, consistent variations rather than random changes, or focus on more impactful ways to enhance the user experience.

explanation: The story lacks a clear user persona and the 'so that' clause, making the tangible business or user value unclear. It describes a technical change to user loading and re-assignment rules rather than a user-centric outcome.
suggested_fix: Rephrase as a user story (e.g., 'As a Requester, I want to be able to re-assign requests only if I am a Workflow or Main Admin, so that re-assignment permissions are properly enforced.') or clarify the specific user benefit of excluding the V2 Approval List.

explanation: The story lacks a clear user persona and the 'so that' clause, making the tangible business or user value unclear. The first point is ambiguous about 'unknown notification options,' and the second point lists a massive set of technical features without explaining the user's goal or benefit.
suggested_fix: Rephrase as a user story (e.g., 'As an Admin, I want to configure advanced email notification settings for grouped resource publish templates, so that users receive relevant and timely updates.') and clarify the specific user needs for each feature listed.

explanation: The story lacks a clear user persona and the 'so that' clause, making the tangible business or user value unclear. It describes a technical rule for displaying a 'Decline' option under specific conditions rather than a user-centric outcome.
suggested_fix: Rephrase as a user story (e.g., 'As a Reviewer, I want the 'Decline' option to always be visible in the Bulk Review panel, even if some selected items have 'Hide Decline' enabled, so that I can consistently decline applicable items.') or clarify the specific user benefit of this behavior.

explanation: The story lacks a clear user persona and the 'so that' clause, making the tangible business or user value unclear. It describes a technical need for a 'mechanism' to remove facial data rather than a user-centric goal or outcome.
suggested_fix: Rephrase as a user story (e.g., 'As an Admin, I want to be able to completely remove all existing facial data and related flags from an MCP Site, so that I can ensure data privacy and reset facial recognition for a cloned site.')
